scenes.text: In this lecture, we will explore Docker, a platform designed to help developers build, deploy, and run applications within containers. Docker containers allow applications to run consistently across different environments, reducing the 'it works on my machine' problem. This scene will cover the key components of Docker, including Docker Engine, Docker Images, and Docker Containers, and discuss how they work together to streamline the application deployment process.
scenes.image_prompt: graph TD; A[Docker Engine] --> B[Docker Images]; A --> C[Docker Containers]; B --> D[Application Code & Dependencies]; B --> E[Configuration Settings]; C --> F[Isolated Environment]; C --> G[Consistent Operation Across Platforms]; F -.-> H[Developer Environment]; G -.-> I[Production Environment];
